latest.content:
23 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9908T
Trustpilot Group PLC
23 February 2026
23 February 2026
Trustpilot Group plc
Transaction in own shares
Trustpilot Group plc ("Trustpilot" or the "Company")
announces that on 20 February 2026
,
it purchased the following number of its ordinary shares of £0.01 each (the "Ordinary Shares") on the London Stock Exchange from
Deutsche Bank AG, London Branch (trading for these purposes as Deutsche Numis) ("Deutsche Numis")
, in accordance with the terms of the share buyback programme announced on
16 September 2025
(the "Buyback Programme").
Date of purchase:
20 February 2026
Aggregate number of Ordinary Shares purchased:
600,000
Lowest price paid per share (GBp):
138.00
Highest price paid per share (GBp):
142.50
Volume weighted average price paid per share (GBp):
140.21
Trustpilot intends to cancel all of the purchased shares.
Since 16 September 2025, the Company has purchased 19,177,896 Ordinary Shares for cancellation, at a cost (excluding dealing and associated costs) of ~£34,540,519.
In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (as incorporated into UK domestic law by the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018), a schedule of individual trades made by Deutsche Numis is available using the following link:
http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/9908T_1-2026-2-22.pdf

company_30s.what_they_do: Trustpilot runs the world’s biggest online review platform, letting consumers rate and review businesses from local shops to global brands. The service powers millions of customer‑generated reviews that influence buying decisions and brand reputations.
company_30s.why_they_matter: With 64 million active users and 300 million reviews, the data Trustpilot collects is a goldmine for marketers, investors and regulators. The platform sits at the crossroads of e‑commerce, digital trust and consumer protection, giving it a moat that’s hard to replicate.
company_30s.current_state: The company is moving from a high‑growth, cash‑burn phase into a more mature, buy‑back‑focused cycle. It has just announced a new share‑buyback extension, reported 18 % year‑over‑year bookings growth for FY 25, and is reviewing a block listing status. The stock is trading far below its historical highs, suggesting a deep market‑price drop.

rns_narrative: Trustpilot is tightening its governance by swapping out directors and adjusting share‑holding structures, signalling a desire for clearer control.
The company is aggressively buying back shares and extending the buy‑back programme, a classic move to support the price when fundamentals remain solid.

Financially, FY 25 bookings grew 18 %, and the firm has a strong cash position that backs the buy‑back. The block listing review indicates the board is keeping the company on the market while smoothing out liquidity concerns.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet debate – a few traders whisper optimism around buybacks, while others voice worry about volatility and a lack of clear catalysts.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the buy‑back extension, strong bookings growth and the company’s cash‑positive balance sheet as reasons to expect a price rally.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears highlight the recent share‑sell activity by institutional holders, the absence of fresh product launches and the possibility that the market will stay pitted on a low support level.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat – no new news has shifted opinions, leaving the crowd largely silent but slightly split.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead, meaning there is almost no public chatter. That is a green light for a contrarian play because a quiet market can surprise when the right catalyst arrives.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings release – if results exceed analyst expectations, it could ignite a bounce.
Any announcement that the block listing review is cleared or that a new tranche of the buy‑back is released.
A volume spike that breaks the current 100‑p support level, signalling renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained 2‑week uptick in daily volume, a break above the 100‑p support with at least 1 million shares traded, and a positive earnings surprise. These signals would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early recovery phase after a deep sell‑off. APEX compression suggests the price is tightening, so a breakout could happen soon if the catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the price recovers to the 100‑p support level, the upside to the next key resistance around 120 p is roughly 20 %. With a buy‑back program that has already removed shares, the supply pressure is easing, which can amplify the move.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The price could stay stuck below 100 p if earnings disappoint or if the buy‑back program stalls. A continued lack of new product traction could keep the stock languishing, leaving a realistic downside of about 20 % from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss capped at 20 % versus upside of 20 % gives a 1:1 risk‑reward. If the price hits the 120‑p level, the reward jumps to 20 % with the same 20 % risk – a 1:1 ratio. The asymmetry is modest because the upside is only a few percentage points, but the risk of a permanent floor is low.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce above 100 p with a stop just below 90 p. Size the position so that a 20 % move would represent no more than 5 % of your portfolio. If the price hits 115 p, consider scaling in or tightening the stop to 95 p.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until either the earnings release shows a positive surprise or a volume spike breaks the 100‑p support with at least 1 million shares traded. Only then move from a watch position to a trade.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified. The only scenario that would force a pass is if the company announces a delisting or a major regulatory investigation that would crush the share price.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Trustpilot’s deep crash and strong buy‑back activity create a modest contrarian upside if earnings or a listing review clears, but the lack of fresh catalysts keeps the risk moderate.
